sentences.zh: 韓之先與周同姓，姓姬氏。

translations.literal: The Han ancestors shared the same surname as the Zhou royal house, the Ji clan.
translations.idiomatic: The ancestors of the Han shared the same surname as the Zhou royal house—they were of the Ji clan.

sentences.zh: 其後苗裔事晉，得封於韓原，曰韓武子。

translations.literal: Later descendants served Jin, obtained enfeoffment at Han Yuan, and were called Han Wu Zi.
translations.idiomatic: Their later descendants served the state of Jin and received a fief at Hanyuan. The first lord of that line was known as Han Wuzi.

sentences.zh: 武子後三世有韓厥，從封姓為韓氏。

translations.literal: Three generations after Wu Zi there was Han Jue, who from his enfeoffment took the surname Han clan.
translations.idiomatic: Three generations after Wuzi, there arose Han Jue, who took the surname Han from his fief.

sentences.zh: 韓厥，晉景公之三年，晉司寇屠岸賈將作亂，誅靈公之賊趙盾。

translations.literal: Han Jue, in Jin Jing Gong's third year, the Jin Si Kou Tu An Jia was about to rebel and execute Ling Gong's traitor Zhao Dun.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year of Duke Jing of Jin, the Minister of Justice Tu'an Jia plotted to carry out a purge, intending to punish Zhao Dun for the assassination of Duke Ling.

sentences.zh: 趙盾已死矣，欲誅其子趙朔。

translations.literal: Zhao Dun was already dead, and they desired to execute his son Zhao Shuo.
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Dun was already dead, so Tu'an Jia sought to execute his son, Zhao Shuo.

sentences.zh: 韓厥止賈，賈不聽。

translations.literal: Han Jue tried to stop Jia, but Jia would not listen.
translations.idiomatic: Han Jue tried to dissuade him, but Tu'an Jia would not listen.

sentences.zh: 厥告趙朔令亡。

translations.literal: Jue told Zhao Shuo to flee.
translations.idiomatic: Han Jue warned Zhao Shuo and urged him to flee.

sentences.zh: 朔曰：「子必能不絕趙祀，死不恨矣。」

translations.literal: Shuo said: 'Sir must able not extinguish Zhao sacrifices, die not regret.'
translations.idiomatic: Zhao Shuo said, 'If you can ensure that the Zhao sacrifices are not extinguished, I will die without regret.'

sentences.zh: 韓厥許之。

translations.literal: Han Jue promised him.
translations.idiomatic: Han Jue gave him his word.

sentences.zh: 及賈誅趙氏，厥稱疾不出。

translations.literal: When Jia executed the Zhao clan, Jue claimed illness and did not go out.
translations.idiomatic: When Tu'an Jia massacred the Zhao clan, Han Jue feigned illness and stayed away.

sentences.zh: 程嬰、公孫杵臼之藏趙孤趙武也，厥知之。

translations.literal: Cheng Ying and Gong Sun Chu Jiu hid the Zhao orphan Zhao Wu, and Jue knew it.
translations.idiomatic: When Cheng Ying and Gongsun Chujiu hid the orphan Zhao Wu, Han Jue knew of it.

sentences.zh: 景公十一年，厥與郤克將兵八百乘伐齊，敗齊頃公于鞍，獲逢丑父。

translations.literal: In Jing Gong's eleventh year, Jue and Xi Ke led eight hundred chariots to attack Qi, defeated Qi Qing Gong at An, and captured Feng Chou Fu.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh year of Duke Jing, Han Jue and Xi Ke led an army of eight hundred chariots against Qi, defeated Duke Qing of Qi at the Battle of An, and captured Feng Choufu.

sentences.zh: 於是晉作六卿，而韓厥在一卿之位，號為獻子。

translations.literal: Thus Jin established the six ministers, and Han Jue held one of the ministerial positions, called Xian Zi.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, Jin established the system of six ministers, and Han Jue held one of those positions. He was posthumously styled Xianzi.

sentences.zh: 晉景公十七年，病，卜大業之不遂者為祟。

translations.literal: In Jin Jing Gong's seventeenth year, he fell ill. They divined that spirits were causing harm because great enterprises were not succeeding.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventeenth year of Duke Jing of Jin, the duke fell ill. A divination revealed that the spirits of those whose great works had been cut short were causing the affliction.

sentences.zh: 韓厥稱趙成季之功，今後無祀，以感景公。

translations.literal: Han Jue cited Zhao Cheng Ji's merits, saying that now his descendants had no sacrifices, to move Jing Gong.
translations.idiomatic: Han Jue spoke of the merits of Zhao Chengji, pointing out that his descendants now had no one to carry on the ancestral sacrifices. This moved Duke Jing deeply.

sentences.zh: 景公問曰：「尚有世乎？」

translations.literal: Jing Gong asked: 'Still have descendants?'
translations.idiomatic: Duke Jing asked, 'Are there still descendants of the Zhao line?'

sentences.zh: 厥於是言趙武，而復與故趙氏田邑，續趙氏祀。

translations.literal: Jue thus spoke of Zhao Wu, restored the former Zhao clan's fields and cities, and continued the Zhao clan sacrifices.
translations.idiomatic: Han Jue then spoke of Zhao Wu, and the former lands and towns of the Zhao clan were restored to him, so that the Zhao ancestral sacrifices might continue.

sentences.zh: 晉悼公之（十）［七］年，韓獻子老。

translations.literal: In Jin Dao Gong's seventh year, Han Xian Zi grew old.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh year of Duke Dao of Jin, Han Xianzi had grown old.

sentences.zh: 獻子卒，子宣子代。

translations.literal: Xian Zi died, and his son Xuan Zi succeeded him.
translations.idiomatic: When Xianzi died, his son Xuanzi succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 宣字徙居州。

translations.literal: Xuan Zi moved his residence to Zhou.
translations.idiomatic: Xuanzi moved his residence to Zhou.

sentences.zh: 晉平公十四年，吳季札使晉，曰：「晉國之政卒歸於韓、魏、趙矣。」

translations.literal: Jin Ping Gong fourteenth year, Wu Ji Zha envoy to Jin, said: 'Jin state governance finally belongs to Han, Wei, Zhao.'
translations.idiomatic: In the fourteenth year of Duke Ping of Jin, Ji Zha of Wu came as an envoy to Jin and declared, 'The governance of Jin will ultimately fall to the houses of Han, Wei, and Zhao.'

sentences.zh: 晉頃公十二年，韓宣子與趙、魏共分祁氏、羊舌氏十縣。

translations.literal: In Jin Qing Gong's twelfth year, Han Xuan Zi, with Zhao and Wei, together divided the ten counties of the Qi clan and Yang She clan.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth year of Duke Qing of Jin, Han Xuanzi, together with the Zhao and Wei houses, divided among themselves the ten counties belonging to the Qi and Yangshe clans.

sentences.zh: 晉定公十五年，宣子與趙簡子侵伐范、中行氏。

translations.literal: In Jin Ding Gong's fifteenth year, Xuan Zi and Zhao Jian Zi invaded and attacked the Fan and Zhong Hang clans.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifteenth year of Duke Ding of Jin, Xuanzi and Zhao Jianzi launched an attack against the Fan and Zhonghang clans.

sentences.zh: 宣子卒，子貞子代立。

translations.literal: Xuan Zi died, and his son Zhen Zi succeeded and was established.
translations.idiomatic: When Xuanzi died, his son Zhenzi succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 貞子徙居平陽。

translations.literal: Zhen Zi moved his residence to Ping Yang.
translations.idiomatic: Zhenzi moved his residence to Pingyang.

sentences.zh: 貞子卒，子簡子代。

translations.literal: Zhen Zi died, and his son Jian Zi succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhenzi died, his son Jianzi succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 簡子卒，子莊子代。

translations.literal: Jian Zi died, and his son Zhuang Zi succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Jianzi died, his son Zhuangzi succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 莊子卒，子康子代。

translations.literal: Zhuang Zi died, and his son Kang Zi succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Zhuangzi died, his son Kangzi succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 康子與趙襄子、魏桓子共敗知伯，分其地，地益大，大於諸侯。

translations.literal: Kang Zi, with Zhao Xiang Zi and Wei Huan Zi, together defeated Zhi Bo, divided his territory, and their land became increasingly great, greater than the other feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: Kangzi, together with Zhao Xiangzi and Wei Huanzi, defeated Zhi Bo, divided his territory among themselves, and so their domains grew ever larger—surpassing those of many feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 康子卒，子武子代。

translations.literal: Kang Zi died, and his son Wu Zi succeeded.
translations.idiomatic: When Kangzi died, his son Wuzi succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 武子二年，伐鄭，殺其君幽公。

translations.literal: In Wu Zi's second year, they attacked Zheng and killed their lord You Gong.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of Wuzi, they attacked Zheng and killed its lord, Duke You.

sentences.zh: 十六年，武子卒，子景侯立。

translations.literal: In the sixteenth year, Wu Zi died, and his son Jing Hou was established.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixteenth year, Wuzi died and his son, Marquis Jing, came to power.

sentences.zh: 景侯虔元年，伐鄭，取雍丘。

translations.literal: In Jing Hou Qian's first year, they attacked Zheng and took Yong Qiu.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of Marquis Jing Qian, they attacked Zheng and captured Yongqiu.

sentences.zh: 二年，鄭敗我負黍。

translations.literal: In the second year, Zheng defeated us at Fu Shu.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year, Zheng defeated our forces at Fushu.

sentences.zh: 六年，與趙、魏俱得列為諸侯。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, with Zhao and Wei they together obtained recognition as feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year, Han, together with Zhao and Wei, were all formally recognized as feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 九年，鄭圍我陽翟。

translations.literal: In the ninth year, Zheng besieged our Yang Di.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth year, Zheng besieged our city of Yangdi.

sentences.zh: 景侯卒，子列侯取立。

translations.literal: Jing Hou died, and his son Lie Hou Qu was established.
translations.idiomatic: When Marquis Jing died, his son Marquis Lie Qu came to power.

sentences.zh: 列侯三年，聶政殺韓相俠累。

translations.literal: In Lie Hou's third year, Nie Zheng killed the Han chancellor Xia Lei.
translations.idiomatic: In the third year of Marquis Lie, the assassin Nie Zheng killed the Han chancellor Xia Lei.

sentences.zh: 九年，秦伐我宜陽，取六邑。

translations.literal: In the ninth year, Qin attacked our Yi Yang and took six cities.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth year, Qin attacked our Yiyang and seized six towns.

sentences.zh: 十三年，列侯卒，子文侯立。

translations.literal: In the thirteenth year, Lie Hou died, and his son Wen Hou was established.
translations.idiomatic: In the thirteenth year, Marquis Lie died and his son, Marquis Wen, succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 是歲魏文侯卒。

translations.literal: This year Wei Wen Hou died.
translations.idiomatic: That same year, Marquis Wen of Wei also died.

sentences.zh: 文侯二年，伐鄭，取陽城。

translations.literal: In Wen Hou's second year, they attacked Zheng and took Yang Cheng.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of Marquis Wen, they attacked Zheng and captured Yangcheng.

sentences.zh: 伐宋，到彭城，執宋君。

translations.literal: They attacked Song, arrived at Peng Cheng, and seized the Song jun.
translations.idiomatic: They attacked Song, marched to Pengcheng, and captured the lord of Song.

sentences.zh: 七年，伐齊，至桑丘。

translations.literal: In the seventh year, they attacked Qi and arrived at Sang Qiu.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh year, they attacked Qi and advanced as far as Sangqiu.

sentences.zh: 鄭反晉。

translations.literal: Zheng rebelled against Jin.
translations.idiomatic: Zheng revolted against Jin.

sentences.zh: 九年，伐齊，至靈丘。

translations.literal: In the ninth year, they attacked Qi and arrived at Ling Qiu.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth year, they attacked Qi and advanced as far as Lingqiu.

sentences.zh: 十年，文侯卒，子哀侯立。

translations.literal: In the tenth year, Wen Hou died, and his son Ai Hou was established.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth year, Marquis Wen died and his son, Marquis Ai, succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 哀侯元年，與趙、魏分晉國。

translations.literal: In Ai Hou's first year, with Zhao and Wei they divided the Jin state.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of Marquis Ai, Han, together with Zhao and Wei, partitioned the state of Jin.

sentences.zh: 二年，滅鄭，因徙都鄭。

translations.literal: In the second year, they extinguished Zheng, and thus moved their capital to Zheng.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year, they conquered Zheng and moved the Han capital there.

sentences.zh: 六年，韓嚴弒其君哀侯。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, Han Yan assassinated his lord Ai Hou.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year, Han Yan assassinated his sovereign, Marquis Ai.

sentences.zh: 而子懿侯立。

translations.literal: And his son Yi Hou was established.
translations.idiomatic: His son, Marquis Yi, was then established as ruler.

sentences.zh: 懿侯二年，魏敗我馬陵。

translations.literal: In Yi Hou's second year, Wei defeated us at Ma Ling.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of Marquis Yi, Wei defeated our forces at Maling.

sentences.zh: 五年，與魏惠王會宅陽。

translations.literal: In the fifth year, they met with Wei Hui Wang at Zhai Yang.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year, they met with King Hui of Wei at Zhaiyang.

sentences.zh: 九年，魏敗我澮。

translations.literal: In the ninth year, Wei defeated us at Kuai.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth year, Wei defeated our forces at Kuai.

sentences.zh: 十二年，懿侯卒，子昭侯立。

translations.literal: In the twelfth year, Yi Hou died, and his son Zhao Hou was established.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth year, Marquis Yi died and his son, Marquis Zhao, succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 昭侯元年，秦敗我西山。

translations.literal: In Zhao Hou's first year, Qin defeated us at Xi Shan.
translations.idiomatic: In the first year of Marquis Zhao, Qin defeated our forces at Xishan.

sentences.zh: 二年，宋取我黃池。

translations.literal: In the second year, Song took our Huang Chi.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year, Song captured our city of Huangchi.

sentences.zh: 魏取朱。

translations.literal: Wei took Zhu.
translations.idiomatic: Wei seized Zhu.

sentences.zh: 六年，伐東周，取陵觀、邢丘。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, they attacked Eastern Zhou and took Ling Guan and Xing Qiu.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year, they attacked Eastern Zhou and captured Lingguan and Xingqiu.

sentences.zh: 八年，申不害相韓，修術行道，國內以治，諸侯不來侵伐。

translations.literal: In the eighth year, Shen Buhai became chancellor of Han. He cultivated techniques and practiced the way. Within the state there was order, and the feudal lords did not come to invade and attack.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth year, Shen Buhai became chancellor of Han. He cultivated the art of statecraft and put good governance into practice. The state was well-ordered, and none of the feudal lords dared to invade.

sentences.zh: 十年，韓姬弒其君悼公。

translations.literal: In the tenth year, Han Ji assassinated his lord Dao Gong.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth year, Han Ji assassinated his lord, Duke Dao.

sentences.zh: 十一年，昭侯如秦。

translations.literal: In the eleventh year, Zhao Hou went to Qin.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh year, Marquis Zhao traveled to Qin.

sentences.zh: 二十二年，申不害死。

translations.literal: In the twenty-second year, Shen Buhai died.
translations.idiomatic: In the twenty-second year, Shen Buhai died.

sentences.zh: 二十四年，秦來拔我宜陽。

translations.literal: In the twenty-fourth year, Qin came and captured our Yi Yang.
translations.idiomatic: In the twenty-fourth year, Qin came and captured our city of Yiyang.

sentences.zh: 二十五年，旱，作高門。

translations.literal: In the twenty-fifth year, there was drought, and they made a high gate.
translations.idiomatic: In the twenty-fifth year, there was a drought, yet the marquis ordered the construction of a grand gate.

sentences.zh: 屈宜臼曰：「昭侯不出此門。

translations.literal: Qu Yu Jiu said: 'Zhao Hou not exit this gate.
translations.idiomatic: Qu Yijiu said, 'Marquis Zhao will never pass through this gate.

sentences.zh: 何也？

translations.literal: Why?
translations.idiomatic: Why do I say this?

sentences.zh: 不時。

translations.literal: Not timely.
translations.idiomatic: Because the timing is wrong.

sentences.zh: 吾所謂時者，非時日也，人固有利不利時。

translations.literal: What I call timely is not time and day. People indeed have favorable and unfavorable timing.
translations.idiomatic: By timing, I do not mean the calendar. Rather, every person has seasons of fortune and misfortune.

sentences.zh: 昭侯嘗利矣，不作高門。

translations.literal: Zhao Hou was once favorable. He did not make a high gate.
translations.idiomatic: When Marquis Zhao enjoyed good fortune, he did not build a grand gate.

sentences.zh: 往年秦拔宜陽，今年旱，昭侯不以此時卹民之急，而顧益奢，此謂『時絀舉贏』。」

translations.literal: Previous year Qin captured Yi Yang, this year drought, Zhao Hou not with this time relieve people's urgency, but moreover increase luxury, this called 'timing deficient undertake surplus'.'
translations.idiomatic: Last year Qin captured Yiyang; this year there is a drought. Yet rather than relieving the people's hardship in this time of crisis, the marquis indulges in ever greater extravagance. This is what is called spending lavishly when the times demand frugality.'

sentences.zh: 二十六年，高門成，昭侯卒，果不出此門。

translations.literal: In the twenty-sixth year, the high gate was completed. Zhao Hou died, and indeed did not exit this gate.
translations.idiomatic: In the twenty-sixth year, the grand gate was completed. Marquis Zhao died and, as predicted, never once passed through it.

sentences.zh: 子宣惠王立。

translations.literal: His son Xuan Hui Wang was established.
translations.idiomatic: His son, King Xuan Hui, succeeded him.

sentences.zh: 宣惠王五年，張儀相秦。

translations.literal: In Xuan Hui Wang's fifth year, Zhang Yi became chancellor of Qin.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year of King Xuan Hui, Zhang Yi became chancellor of Qin.

sentences.zh: 八年，魏敗我將韓舉。

translations.literal: In the eighth year, Wei defeated our general Han Ju.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth year, Wei defeated our general Han Ju.

sentences.zh: 十一年，君號為王。

translations.literal: In the eleventh year, the lord was titled wang.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh year, the lord assumed the title of king.

sentences.zh: 與趙會區鼠。

translations.literal: They met with Zhao at Qu Shu.
translations.idiomatic: He met with Zhao at Qushu.

sentences.zh: 十四，秦伐敗我鄢。

translations.literal: In the fourteenth year, Qin attacked and defeated us at Yan.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourteenth year, Qin attacked and defeated us at Yan.

sentences.zh: 十六年，秦敗我修魚，虜得韓將鯁、申差於濁澤。

translations.literal: In the sixteenth year, Qin defeated us at Xiu Yu and captured the Han generals Geng and Shen Chai at Zhu Ze.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixteenth year, Qin defeated us at Xiuyu and captured the Han generals Geng and Shen Chai at Zhuoze.

sentences.zh: 韓氏急，公仲謂韓王曰：「與國非可恃也。

translations.literal: Han clan urgent, Gong Zhong told Han wang said: 'Allied states not reliable.
translations.idiomatic: The state of Han was in dire straits. Gong Zhong said to the King of Han, 'Our allies cannot be relied upon.

sentences.zh: 今秦之欲伐楚久矣，王不如因張儀為和於秦，賂以一名都，具甲，與之南伐楚，此以一易二之計也。」

translations.literal: Now Qin desire attack Chu long time, wang better through Zhang Yi make peace with Qin, bribe with one famous city, prepare armor, with them south attack Chu, this exchange one for two strategy.'
translations.idiomatic: Qin has long wished to attack Chu. Your Majesty would do better to make peace with Qin through Zhang Yi, offer them one great city as tribute, arm our forces, and join them in a southern campaign against Chu. This is the strategy of trading one to gain two.'

sentences.zh: 韓王曰：「善。」

translations.literal: Han wang said: 'Good.'
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han said, 'Very well.'

sentences.zh: 乃警公仲之行，將西購於秦。

translations.literal: Thus they prepared Gong Zhong's journey, about to negotiate with Qin in the west.
translations.idiomatic: They then prepared Gong Zhong's mission, intending to negotiate terms with Qin in the west.

sentences.zh: 楚王聞之大恐，召陳軫告之。

translations.literal: The Chu wang heard this and was greatly fearful. He summoned Chen Zhen and told him.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Chu learned of this, he was greatly alarmed. He summoned Chen Zhen and informed him.

sentences.zh: 陳軫曰：「秦之欲伐楚久矣，今又得韓之名都一而具甲，秦韓并兵而伐楚，此秦所禱祀而求也。

translations.literal: Chen Zhen said: 'Qin desire attack Chu long time, now moreover get Han famous city one prepare armor, Qin Han combine troops attack Chu, this Qin pray sacrifice seek.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Zhen said, 'Qin has long desired to attack Chu. Now they have also obtained a great city from Han and armed their forces. If Qin and Han combine their armies to invade Chu, it will be the very thing Qin has prayed to the gods for.

sentences.zh: 今已得之矣，楚國必伐矣。

translations.literal: Now they have already obtained it. Chu will certainly be attacked.'
translations.idiomatic: Now that they have obtained it, Chu will certainly be invaded.

sentences.zh: 王聽臣為之警四境之內，起師言救韓，命戰車滿道路，發信臣，多其車，重其幣，使信王之救己也。

translations.literal: Your Majesty listen to this minister and for it alert within the four borders, raise troops saying they save Han, order chariots to fill the roads, send trusted ministers with many chariots and heavy gifts, making them believe the wang will save them.'
translations.idiomatic: If Your Majesty heeds my counsel, alert the troops within our borders, raise an army under the banner of rescuing Han, fill the roads with war chariots, and dispatch trusted envoys with lavish gifts—so that Han will believe Your Majesty intends to come to their aid.

sentences.zh: 縱韓不能聽我，韓必德王也，必不為鴈行以來，是秦韓不和也，兵雖至，楚不大病也。

translations.literal: Even if Han cannot listen to us, Han will certainly virtue the wang and will not come in wild goose formation. This means Qin and Han are not harmonious. Though troops arrive, Chu will not be greatly harmed.'
translations.idiomatic: Even if Han does not heed us, they will be grateful to Your Majesty and will not march in formation alongside Qin. This means Qin and Han will not act in concert. Even if their troops come, Chu will suffer no great harm.

sentences.zh: 為能聽我絕和於秦，秦必大怒，以厚怨韓。

translations.literal: If they can listen to us and cut peace with Qin, Qin will certainly be greatly angry and harbor thick resentment toward Han.'
translations.idiomatic: If Han does heed us and breaks off peace with Qin, then Qin will be furious and bear deep resentment toward Han.

sentences.zh: 韓之南交楚，必輕秦；

translations.literal: Han south ally Chu, must slight Qin;
translations.idiomatic: If Han allies with Chu in the south, they will inevitably slight Qin.

sentences.zh: 輕秦，其應秦必不敬：是因秦、韓之兵而免楚國之患也。」

translations.literal: Slight Qin, their respond Qin must not respectful: this through Qin, Han troops avoid Chu state calamity.'
translations.idiomatic: If they slight Qin, their response to Qin will surely be disrespectful—and thus we may use the rift between Qin and Han to free Chu from danger.'

sentences.zh: 楚王曰：「善。」

translations.literal: Chu wang said: 'Good.'
translations.idiomatic: The King of Chu said, 'Excellent.'

sentences.zh: 乃警四境之內，興師言救韓。

translations.literal: Thus they alerted within the four borders and raised troops, saying they would save Han.
translations.idiomatic: They then mobilized troops throughout the kingdom and raised an army, proclaiming that they would rescue Han.

sentences.zh: 命戰車滿道路，發信臣，多其車，重其幣。

translations.literal: They ordered chariots to fill the roads and sent trusted ministers with many chariots and heavy gifts.
translations.idiomatic: They ordered war chariots to fill the roads and dispatched trusted envoys with grand retinues and lavish gifts.

sentences.zh: 謂韓王曰：「不穀國雖小，已悉發之矣。

translations.literal: Told Han wang said: 'Unworthy state though small, already all sent.
translations.idiomatic: The envoys told the King of Han, 'Though our humble kingdom is small, we have already mobilized every soldier.

sentences.zh: 願大國遂肆志於秦，不穀將以楚殉韓。」

translations.literal: Wish great state freely vent will on Qin, unworthy will with Chu follow Han.'
translations.idiomatic: We hope Your Majesty's great state will press forward against Qin without restraint. Our humble king will commit all of Chu to Han's cause.'

sentences.zh: 韓王聞之大說，乃止公仲之行。

translations.literal: The Han wang heard this and was greatly pleased. Thus he stopped Gong Zhong's journey.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Han heard this, he was greatly pleased and called off Gong Zhong's mission.

sentences.zh: 公仲曰：「不可。

translations.literal: Gong Zhong said: 'Not acceptable.
translations.idiomatic: Gong Zhong said, 'This must not be done.

sentences.zh: 夫以實伐我者秦也，以虛名救我者楚也。

translations.literal: The one attacking us with reality is Qin. The one saving us with empty name is Chu.'
translations.idiomatic: It is Qin that attacks us with real force; it is Chu that rescues us with empty promises.

sentences.zh: 王恃楚之虛名，而輕絕彊秦之敵，王必為天下大笑。

translations.literal: The wang relying on Chu's empty name, lightly cutting alliance with strong Qin - the wang will certainly become the world's great laughingstock.'
translations.idiomatic: If Your Majesty relies on Chu's empty promises and carelessly breaks with mighty Qin, you will surely become the laughingstock of all under Heaven.

sentences.zh: 且楚韓非兄弟之國也，又非素約而謀伐秦也。

translations.literal: Moreover, Chu and Han are not brother states, and moreover they are not long-time allies who planned to attack Qin.'
translations.idiomatic: Moreover, Chu and Han are not brother states, nor have they been longstanding allies plotting against Qin.

sentences.zh: 已有伐形，因發兵言救韓，此必陳軫之謀也。

translations.literal: They already have the appearance of attacking. Thus they send troops saying they save Han. This must be Chen Zhen's strategy.'
translations.idiomatic: They have already assumed the posture of an invader, yet they send troops claiming to rescue Han. This must be a stratagem devised by Chen Zhen.

sentences.zh: 且王已使人報於秦矣，今不行，是欺秦也。

translations.literal: Moreover, the wang has already sent people to report to Qin. Now not going would be deceiving Qin.'
translations.idiomatic: Besides, Your Majesty has already sent word to Qin. To reverse course now would be to deceive them.

sentences.zh: 夫輕欺彊秦而信楚之謀臣，恐王必悔之。」

translations.literal: Lightly deceive strong Qin trust Chu strategist minister, fear wang must regret it.'
translations.idiomatic: To recklessly deceive mighty Qin while trusting a Chu strategist—I fear Your Majesty will come to regret this deeply.'

sentences.zh: 韓王不聽，遂絕於秦。

translations.literal: The Han wang did not listen and thus cut off relations with Qin.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han would not listen, and so he broke off relations with Qin.

sentences.zh: 秦因大怒，益甲伐韓，大戰，楚救不至韓。

translations.literal: Qin thus became greatly angry, increased its armor and attacked Han. There was a great battle, but Chu's rescue did not arrive at Han.
translations.idiomatic: Qin was enraged, reinforced its armies, and attacked Han. A great battle ensued, but the promised Chu relief forces never arrived.

sentences.zh: 十九年，大破我岸門。

translations.literal: In the nineteenth year, they greatly broke our An Men.
translations.idiomatic: In the nineteenth year, they inflicted a devastating defeat on us at Anmen.

sentences.zh: 太子倉質於秦以和。

translations.literal: Crown prince Cang became a hostage in Qin for peace.
translations.idiomatic: The crown prince Cang was sent as a hostage to Qin to sue for peace.

sentences.zh: 二十一年，與秦共攻楚，敗楚將屈丐，斬首八萬於丹陽。

translations.literal: In the twenty-first year, they attacked Chu together with Qin, defeated Chu general Qu Gai, and beheaded eighty thousand at Dan Yang.
translations.idiomatic: In the twenty-first year, they joined Qin in attacking Chu, defeated the Chu general Qu Gai, and took eighty thousand heads at Danyang.

sentences.zh: 是歲，宣惠王卒，太子倉立，是為襄王。

translations.literal: This year, Xuan Hui Wang died. Crown prince Cang was established, called Xiang Wang.
translations.idiomatic: That year, King Xuan Hui died. The crown prince Cang succeeded him and became King Xiang.

sentences.zh: 襄王四年，與秦武王會臨晉。

translations.literal: In Xiang Wang's fourth year, they met with Qin Wu Wang at Lin Jin.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth year of King Xiang, he met with King Wu of Qin at Linjin.

sentences.zh: 其秋，秦使甘茂攻我宜陽。

translations.literal: That autumn, Qin sent Gan Mao to attack our Yi Yang.
translations.idiomatic: That autumn, Qin sent Gan Mao to attack our city of Yiyang.

sentences.zh: 五年，秦拔我宜陽，斬首六萬。

translations.literal: In the fifth year, Qin captured our Yi Yang and beheaded sixty thousand.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year, Qin captured our Yiyang and took sixty thousand heads.

sentences.zh: 秦武王卒。

translations.literal: Qin Wu Wang died.
translations.idiomatic: King Wu of Qin died.

sentences.zh: 六年，秦復與我武遂。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, Qin again gave us Wu Sui.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year, Qin returned Wusui to us.

sentences.zh: 九年，秦復取我武遂。

translations.literal: In the ninth year, Qin again took our Wu Sui.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth year, Qin took Wusui from us once again.

sentences.zh: 十年，太子嬰朝秦而歸。

translations.literal: In the tenth year, crown prince Ying went for audience in Qin and returned.
translations.idiomatic: In the tenth year, the crown prince Ying attended an audience at the Qin court and returned.

sentences.zh: 十一年，秦伐我，取穰。

translations.literal: In the eleventh year, Qin attacked us and took Rang.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh year, Qin attacked us and captured Rang.

sentences.zh: 與秦伐楚，敗楚將唐眛。

translations.literal: They attacked Chu with Qin and defeated Chu general Tang Mei.
translations.idiomatic: They joined Qin in attacking Chu and defeated the Chu general Tang Mei.

sentences.zh: 十二年，太子嬰死。

translations.literal: In the twelfth year, crown prince Ying died.
translations.idiomatic: In the twelfth year, the crown prince Ying died.

sentences.zh: 公子咎、公子蟣蝨爭為太子。

translations.literal: Prince Jiu and Prince Jiu Jie contended to become crown prince.
translations.idiomatic: Prince Jiu and Prince Ji Shi contended for the position of crown prince.

sentences.zh: 時蟣蝨質於楚。

translations.literal: At the time, Jiu Jie was a hostage in Chu.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, Ji Shi was being held as a hostage in Chu.

sentences.zh: 蘇代謂韓咎曰：「蟣蝨亡在楚，楚王欲內之甚。

translations.literal: Su Dai told Han Jiu said: 'Jiu Jie flee in Chu, Chu wang desire take him in very much.
translations.idiomatic: Su Dai said to Han Jiu, 'Ji Shi is an exile in Chu, and the King of Chu is very eager to install him.

sentences.zh: 今楚兵十餘萬在方城之外，公何不令楚王筑萬室之都雍氏之旁，韓必起兵以救之，公必將矣。

translations.literal: Now Chu has over one hundred thousand troops outside Fang Cheng. Why doesn't the duke order the Chu wang to build a city of ten thousand rooms beside Yong Shi? Han will certainly raise troops to save it, and the duke will certainly be the general.'
translations.idiomatic: Now Chu has over a hundred thousand troops beyond Fangcheng. Why not have the King of Chu build a great city beside Yongshi? Han will surely raise an army to counter it, and you, my lord, will certainly be given command of the forces.

sentences.zh: 公因以韓楚之兵奉蟣蝨而內之，其聽公必矣，必以楚韓封公也。」

translations.literal: Duke thus with Han Chu troops support Jiu Jie take him in, he listen duke must, must with Chu Han enfeoff duke.'
translations.idiomatic: Then you can use the combined forces of Han and Chu to escort Ji Shi and install him as heir. He will certainly heed your counsel, and Chu and Han will surely grant you a fief in return.'

sentences.zh: 韓咎從其計。

translations.literal: Han Jiu followed his plan.
translations.idiomatic: Han Jiu followed this plan.

sentences.zh: 楚圍雍氏，韓求救於秦。

translations.literal: Chu besieged Yong Shi, and Han sought rescue from Qin.
translations.idiomatic: Chu besieged Yongshi, and Han appealed to Qin for help.

sentences.zh: 秦未為發，使公孫昧入韓。

translations.literal: Qin had not yet sent troops and sent Gong Sun Mei to enter Han.
translations.idiomatic: Qin had not yet dispatched troops, but sent Gongsun Mei to Han.

sentences.zh: 公仲曰：「子以秦為且救韓乎？」

translations.literal: Gong Zhong said: 'You think Qin about save Han?'
translations.idiomatic: Gong Zhong asked, 'Do you think Qin will actually come to rescue Han?'

sentences.zh: 對曰：「秦王之言曰『請道南鄭、藍田，出兵於楚以待公』，殆不合矣。」

translations.literal: Replied: 'Qin wang words said "request road Nan Zheng, Lan Tian, send troops Chu await duke", probably not proper.'
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Mei replied, 'The King of Qin says he will march through Nanzheng and Lantian, deploy troops against Chu, and await your forces there—but I doubt this will come to pass.'

sentences.zh: 公仲曰：「子以為果乎？」

translations.literal: Gong Zhong said: 'You think truly?'
translations.idiomatic: Gong Zhong asked, 'Do you truly believe that?'

sentences.zh: 對曰：「秦王必祖張儀之故智。

translations.literal: Replied: 'Qin wang must follow Zhang Yi old wisdom.
translations.idiomatic: Gongsun Mei replied, 'The King of Qin will surely follow the old stratagem of Zhang Yi.

sentences.zh: 楚威王攻梁也，張儀謂秦王曰：『與楚攻魏，魏折而入於楚，韓固其與國也，是秦孤也。

translations.literal: Chu Wei Wang attack Liang time, Zhang Yi told Qin wang said: 'With Chu attack Wei, Wei break enter Chu, Han firmly its ally state, this Qin isolated.
translations.idiomatic: When King Wei of Chu attacked Liang, Zhang Yi told the King of Qin, "If we join Chu in attacking Wei, Wei will break and submit to Chu; Han, being Wei's natural ally, will follow suit—and Qin will be left isolated.

sentences.zh: 不如出兵以到之，魏楚大戰，秦取西河之外以歸。』

translations.literal: Not as send troops arrive it, Wei Chu great battle, Qin take Xi He outside return.'
translations.idiomatic: Better to deploy troops as a show of force. While Wei and Chu exhaust themselves in battle, Qin can seize the lands beyond the West River and return home.'

sentences.zh: 今其狀陽言與韓，其實陰善楚。

translations.literal: Now their appearance openly says alliance with Han, but actually secretly favors Chu.
translations.idiomatic: Now Qin outwardly professes alliance with Han, but in truth secretly favors Chu.

sentences.zh: 公待秦而到，必輕與楚戰。

translations.literal: The duke waits for Qin to arrive and will certainly lightly battle with Chu.'

sentences.zh: 楚陰得秦之不用也，必易與公相支也。

translations.literal: Chu secretly gets that Qin will not be used and will certainly easily support the duke.'

sentences.zh: 公戰而勝楚，遂與公乘楚，施三川而歸。

translations.literal: The duke battles and wins against Chu, thus rides with the duke on Chu, bestows San Chuan and returns.'

sentences.zh: 公戰不勝楚，楚塞三川守之，公不能救也。

translations.literal: The duke battles but does not win against Chu. Chu blocks and guards San Chuan. The duke cannot save it.'

sentences.zh: 竊為公患之。

translations.literal: I secretly worry for the duke about it.'

sentences.zh: 司馬庚三反於郢，甘茂與昭魚遇於商於，其言收璽，實類有約也。」

translations.literal: Si Ma Geng three times return Ying, Gan Mao with Zhao Yu meet Shang Yu, their words take seal, actually similar have agreement.'

sentences.zh: 公仲恐，曰：「然則柰何？」

translations.literal: Gong Zhong feared, said: 'Then how?'

sentences.zh: 曰：「公必先韓而後秦，先身而後張儀。

translations.literal: Said: 'Duke must first Han then Qin, first self then Zhang Yi.

sentences.zh: 公不如亟以國合於齊楚，齊楚必委國於公。

translations.literal: The duke should quickly unite the state with Qi and Chu. Qi and Chu will certainly entrust the state to the duke.'

sentences.zh: 公之所惡者張儀也，其實猶不無秦也。」

translations.literal: Duke what hate Zhang Yi, actually still not without Qin.'

sentences.zh: 於是楚解雍氏圍。

translations.literal: Thus Chu lifted the siege of Yong Shi.

sentences.zh: 蘇代又謂秦太后弟羋戎曰：「公叔伯嬰恐秦楚之內蟣蝨也，公何不為韓求質子於楚？

translations.literal: Su Dai again told Qin queen mother younger brother Mi Yong said: 'Gong Shu Bo Ying fear Qin Chu take in Jiu Jie, duke why not for Han seek hostage from Chu?

sentences.zh: 楚王聽入質子於韓，則公叔伯嬰知秦楚之不以蟣蝨為事，必以韓合於秦楚。

translations.literal: If the Chu wang listens and sends a hostage to Han, then Gong Shu Bo Ying will know that Qin and Chu do not regard Jiu Jie as the matter, and will certainly unite Han with Qin and Chu.'

sentences.zh: 秦楚挾韓以窘魏，魏氏不敢合於齊，是齊孤也。

translations.literal: Qin and Chu holding Han will distress Wei. The Wei clan will not dare unite with Qi. This isolates Qi.'

sentences.zh: 公又為秦求質子於楚，楚不聽，怨結於韓。

translations.literal: The duke moreover seeks a hostage from Chu for Qin. Chu does not listen, and resentment ties to Han.'

sentences.zh: 韓挾齊魏以圍楚，楚必重公。

translations.literal: Han holding Qi and Wei to surround Chu, Chu will certainly respect the duke.'

sentences.zh: 公挾秦楚之重以積德於韓，公叔伯嬰必以國待公。」

translations.literal: Duke hold Qin Chu importance accumulate virtue Han, Gong Shu Bo Ying must with state await duke.'

sentences.zh: 於是蟣蝨竟不得歸韓。

translations.literal: Thus Jiu Jie finally was not able to return to Han.

sentences.zh: 韓立咎為太子。

translations.literal: Han established Jiu as crown prince.

sentences.zh: 齊、魏王來。

translations.literal: The Qi and Wei wang came.

sentences.zh: 十四年，與齊、魏王共擊秦，至函谷而軍焉。

translations.literal: In the fourteenth year, together with the Qi and Wei wang they attacked Qin, arrived at Han Gu and stationed there.

sentences.zh: 十六年，秦與我河外及武遂。

translations.literal: In the sixteenth year, Qin gave us lands outside the He and Wu Sui.

sentences.zh: 襄王卒，太子咎立，是為釐王。

translations.literal: Xiang Wang died. Crown prince Jiu was established, called Xi Wang.

sentences.zh: 釐王三年，使公孫喜率周、魏攻秦。

translations.literal: In Xi Wang's third year, he sent Gong Sun Xi to lead Zhou and Wei in attacking Qin.

sentences.zh: 秦敗我二十四萬，虜喜伊闕。

translations.literal: Qin defeated our two hundred forty thousand and captured Xi at Yi Que.

sentences.zh: 五年，秦拔我宛。

translations.literal: In the fifth year, Qin captured our Wan.

sentences.zh: 六年，與秦武遂地二百里。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, they gave Qin two hundred li of Wu Sui land.

sentences.zh: 十年，秦敗我師于夏山。

translations.literal: In the tenth year, Qin defeated our army at Xia Shan.

sentences.zh: 十二年，與秦昭王會西周而佐秦攻齊。

translations.literal: In the twelfth year, they met with Qin Zhao Wang at Xi Zhou and assisted Qin in attacking Qi.

sentences.zh: 齊敗，湣王出亡。

translations.literal: Qi was defeated, and Min Wang fled.

sentences.zh: 十四年，與秦會兩周閒。

translations.literal: In the fourteenth year, they met with Qin between the two Zhou.

sentences.zh: 二十一年，使暴捐救魏，為秦所敗，捐走開封。

translations.literal: In the twenty-first year, he sent Bao Juan to save Wei. He was defeated by Qin, and Juan fled to Kai Feng.

sentences.zh: 二十三年，趙、魏攻我華陽。

translations.literal: In the twenty-third year, Zhao and Wei attacked our Hua Yang.

sentences.zh: 韓告急於秦，秦不救。

translations.literal: Han reported urgency to Qin, but Qin did not save them.

sentences.zh: 韓相國謂陳筮曰：「事急，願公雖病，為一宿之行。」

translations.literal: Han chancellor told Chen Shi said: 'Matter urgent, wish duke though ill, make one night journey.'

sentences.zh: 陳筮見穰侯。

translations.literal: Chen Shi saw Rang Hou.

sentences.zh: 穰侯曰：「事急乎？

translations.literal: Rang Hou said: 'Matter urgent?

sentences.zh: 故使公來。」

translations.literal: Thus sent duke come.'

sentences.zh: 陳筮曰：「未急也。」

translations.literal: Chen Shi said: 'Not yet urgent.'

sentences.zh: 穰侯怒曰：「是可以為公之主使乎？

translations.literal: Rang Hou angry said: 'This can be duke lord send?

sentences.zh: 夫冠蓋相望，告敝邑甚急，公來言未急，何也？」

translations.literal: Carriages mutually see, report humble city very urgent, duke come say not urgent, why?'

sentences.zh: 陳筮曰：「彼韓急則將變而佗從，以未急，故復來耳。」

translations.literal: Chen Shi said: 'That Han urgent then will change other follow, because not urgent, thus return.'

sentences.zh: 穰侯曰：「公無見王，請今發兵救韓。」

translations.literal: Rang Hou said: 'Duke not see wang, please now send troops save Han.'

sentences.zh: 八日而至，敗趙、魏於華陽之下。

translations.literal: They arrived in eight days and defeated Zhao and Wei below Hua Yang.

sentences.zh: 是歲，釐王卒，子桓惠王立。

translations.literal: This year, Xi Wang died. His son Huan Hui Wang was established.

sentences.zh: 桓惠王元年，伐燕。

translations.literal: In Huan Hui Wang's first year, they attacked Yan.

sentences.zh: 九年，秦拔我陘，城汾旁。

translations.literal: In the ninth year, Qin captured our Xing and built a city beside the Fen.

sentences.zh: 十年，秦擊我於太行，我上黨郡守以上黨郡降趙。

translations.literal: In the tenth year, Qin attacked us at Tai Hang. Our Shang Dang commandery governor surrendered the Shang Dang commandery to Zhao.

sentences.zh: 十四年，秦拔趙上黨，殺馬服子卒四十餘萬於長平。

translations.literal: In the fourteenth year, Qin captured Zhao's Shang Dang and killed over four hundred thousand of Ma Fu Zi's troops at Chang Ping.

sentences.zh: 十七年，秦拔我陽城、負黍。

translations.literal: In the seventeenth year, Qin captured our Yang Cheng and Fu Shu.

sentences.zh: 二十二年，秦昭王卒。

translations.literal: In the twenty-second year, Qin Zhao Wang died.

sentences.zh: 二十四年，秦拔我城皋、滎陽。

translations.literal: In the twenty-fourth year, Qin captured our Cheng Gao and Xing Yang.

sentences.zh: 二十六年，秦悉拔我上黨。

translations.literal: In the twenty-sixth year, Qin completely captured our Shang Dang.

sentences.zh: 二十九年，秦拔我十三城。

translations.literal: In the twenty-ninth year, Qin captured thirteen of our cities.

sentences.zh: 三十四年，桓惠王卒，子王安立。

translations.literal: In the thirty-fourth year, Huan Hui Wang died. His son Wang An was established.

sentences.zh: 王安五年，秦攻韓，韓急，使韓非使秦，秦留非，因殺之。

translations.literal: In Wang An's fifth year, Qin attacked Han. Han was urgent and sent Han Fei as envoy to Qin. Qin detained Fei and thus killed him.

sentences.zh: 九年，秦虜王安，盡入其地，為潁州郡。

translations.literal: In the ninth year, Qin captured Wang An, completely entered their territory, and made it the Ying province commandery.

sentences.zh: 韓遂亡。

translations.literal: Han thus perished.

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：韓厥之感晉景公，紹趙孤之子武，以成程嬰、公孫杵臼之義，此天下之陰德也。

translations.literal: Grand Historian said: Han Jue moved Jin Jing Gong, continued Zhao orphan son Wu, completed Cheng Ying, Gong Sun Chu Jiu righteousness, this world hidden virtue.

sentences.zh: 韓氏之功，於晉未睹其大者也。

translations.literal: The Han clan's merits in Jin were not seen as great.

sentences.zh: 然與趙、魏終為諸侯十餘世，宜乎哉！

translations.literal: Yet with Zhao, Wei finally became feudal lords ten remainder generations, fitting!
